No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to eradicating poverty or its specific targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to ending hunger, food security, or nutrition.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to health, well-being, or medical services.

Quality Education.reason: The course is a PhD-level seminar (Target 4.3) focused on enhancing graduate students' research ability (Target 4.4) and includes identifying sustainability issues (Target 4.7).

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to energy access, renewables, or efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: As an 'International Business' seminar, it relates to economic productivity (Target 8.2). The competency on 'socially responsible issues' can cover decent work (Target 8.8).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's focus on 'latest research direction & results' and enhancing 'research and publication capability' directly supports scientific research and innovation (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The competency to identify 'ethical' and 'socially responsible issues' in business could tangentially relate to reducing inequalities of outcome (Target 10.3).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to urban development or community sustainability.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core competency B explicitly requires understanding 'sustainability issues,' aligning with encouraging sustainable business practices and reporting (Target 12.6).

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' are mentioned, there's no explicit focus on climate change mitigation, adaptation, or resilience in the syllabus.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or deforestation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The focus on 'ethical issues' relates to reducing corruption (Target 16.5), and 'Respect Copyright' supports the rule of law (Target 16.3).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's 'academic exchange platform' inviting 'experts and scholars' fosters knowledge-sharing partnerships (Target 17.16) in international business.
evidence: 邀請專家學者進行專題演講
